body:
## Summary
- mount a shared /api router in the backend entrypoint
- implement GET /api/leaderboard with sorting and pagination

comments.body:
Overall the logic is solid — good use of $addFields + $sort in the aggregation pipeline, and the pagination is correct. A few things to address:

catch should forward to next(err) — The current catch {} silently returns 500 without passing the error to the global error handler, so nothing gets logged. Change it to catch (err) { next(err); } and add next to the function signature.
void next in errorHandler.js — This is a workaround to suppress the ESLint "unused variable" warning, but it's not idiomatic. Rename the parameter to _next instead, which is the conventional way to signal an intentionally unused parameter.
sort default value vs comparison — The default is 'winRate' but the comparison uses 'winrate' (after toLowerCase()). The logic works, but it's a bit confusing. Consider normalizing upfront with a comment, or just use lowercase as the default: sort = 'winrate'.
Error response missing data field — The 400 and 500 responses return { success, message } without a data: null field, which breaks the unified response format used everywhere else.

> Overall the logic is solid — good use of $addFields + $sort in the aggregation pipeline, and the pagination is correct. A few things to address:
> 
> catch should forward to next(err) — The current catch {} silently returns 500 without passing the error to the global error handler, so nothing gets logged. Change it to catch (err) { next(err); } and add next to the function signature. void next in errorHandler.js — This is a workaround to suppress the ESLint "unused variable" warning, but it's not idiomatic. Rename the parameter to _next instead, which is the conventional way to signal an intentionally unused parameter. sort default value vs comparison — The default is 'winRate' but the comparison uses 'winrate' (after toLowerCase()). The logic works, but it's a bit confusing. Consider normalizing upfront with a comment, or just use lowercase as the default: sort = 'winrate'. Error response missing data field — The 400 and 500 responses return { success, message } without a data: null field, which breaks the unified response format used everywhere else.

I got you, and I've fixed these problems.

reviews.body: ata: null has been added, and catch has been changed to next(err). Well done.

title: feat: add leaderboard endpoint
